figures.caption: Figure 1: Schematic overview of HAMNO and its physics-informed extension PI-HAMNO, combining multi-scale operator learning with physics-based regularization.

Figure 2: Rollout relative L2 errors for the full neural-operator comparison on the threedimensional phase-field benchmarks: (a) AC, (b) CH, and (c) SH. The vertically stacked layout compares long-horizon predictive behavior across the governing equations while preserving readability.

Figure 3: Rollout relative L2 errors of the U-shaped baselines for the three-dimensional phasefield benchmarks: (a) AC, (b) CH, and (c) SH. The comparison highlights the effect of hierarchical encoder–decoder structure without adaptive local–global fusion.
Figure 4: Training histories of all compared models, showing (a) total loss and (b) test relative L2 error during training.
Figure 5: Predicted phase-field solutions at t = 100 for the AC, CH, and SH benchmarks, showing reference fields, model predictions, and the HAMNO absolute error.
Figure 6: Free-energy decay and mass conservation for the CH benchmark under different physics weights λ.
Table 1: Ablation study of the pure-physics HAMNO setting (λ = 1), where all reported values are mean relative L2 errors at selected rollout times.
Table 2: OOD evaluation results for the 3D AC equation under three unseen initial-condition distributions controlled by α and τ .
Table 3: OOD evaluation results for the 3D CH equation under unseen initial-condition distributions controlled by α and τ .
Table 4: OOD evaluation results for the 3D SH equation under unseen initial-condition distributions controlled by α and τ .
Table 5: Four-seed robustness summary for selected models on the AC, CH, and SH benchmarks, with Mean ± Std reporting seed statistics of the all-time mean relative L2 error.
Table 6: Ten-seed robustness summary for HAMNO on the AC, CH, and SH benchmarks, with Mean ± Std reporting seed statistics of the all-time mean relative L2 error.
Table 7: Neural-operator hyperparameters used for the 3D phase-field benchmarks.
Table 8: Allen–Cahn (3D) rollout errors and computational cost.
Table 9: Cahn–Hilliard (3D) rollout errors and computational cost.
Table 10: Swift–Hohenberg (3D) rollout errors and computational cost.
Table 11: Summary of the governing phase-field models and dataset parameters.
